source.title: A machine learning approach for reliable prediction of amino acid interactions and its application in the directed evolution of enantioselective enzymes

initial_request.research_problem: Directed evolution of enantioselective enzymes is limited by epistasis, where non-additive effects of mutations impair prediction of improved variants. The goal is to develop a machine learning method that predicts amino acid interactions from sequence and fitness data alone, without requiring 3D structure, to guide the engineering of enantioselective enzymes.
initial_request.known_context: Epistasis impairs predictions in protein engineering; machine learning has not been applied to evolve enzyme mutants with enhanced or inverted stereoselectivity.
input_data: 9 single point mutants of ANEH with experimental E-values
quantifiable_goals: Predict improved enantioselective variants beyond LW202 (E=115)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Epistasis impairs predictions in protein engineering; machine learning not applied to stereoselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to capture non-additive mutational effects without 3D structure?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Digital signal processing (FFT) and protein spectra can model global sequence effects to predict fitness and identify improved mutants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose innov'SAR approach using FFT on amino acid sequences.
evolution_trajectory.observation: FFT-based protein spectrum may capture epistasis by considering whole-sequence impact.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design the innov'SAR methodology with encoding, modelling, and predictive phases.

evidence.quote_or_span: Epistasis phenomena can impair the predictions in protein engineering and screening.
In this sense, machine learning has not been applied in order to evolve enzyme mutants with enhanced or inverted stereoselectivity.

We propose a new approach based only on the amino acids sequence and using digital signal processing (FFT) and protein spectrum to modelling and predict the biological activity/fitness and for identifying combination of single points amino acid substitutions with improved fitness.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that FFT can capture epistasis.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a concrete methodology to encode sequences, build models, and validate predictions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: innov'SAR with three phases (encoding via AAindex + FFT, modelling via PLS regression, predictive phase) can predict fitness.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design innov'SAR: encode sequences using AAindex, apply FFT to get protein spectrum, use PLS regression, validate with LOOCV and R²/RMSE.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Methodology defined; multiple AAindex indices to be evaluated for best model.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Collect initial experimental data (9 single mutants) to build first model.

evidence.quote_or_span: innov'SAR consists of 3 phases, namely, the encoding phase, the modelling phase and the predictive phase.
a Fast Fourier transform is applied to convert the encoded sequence into a protein spectrum
innov'SAR used a partial least square regression, PLS, as algorithm of regression
The method of cross-validation used for this study is the Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV)
n=9 single point mutants of the enzyme were experimentally assessed for their enantioselectivity and used as a learning dataset to build a model.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: innov'SAR design ready; need initial training data.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Lack of experimental fitness data for model building.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Nine single point mutants of ANEH with known enantioselectivity can serve as initial learning set.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use 9 experimentally assessed single point mutants of ANEH as dataset A.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Nine single point mutants with E-values obtained; best mutant LW202 has E=115.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze epistasis in recombinants of these 9 mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: n=9 single point mutants of the enzyme were experimentally assessed for their enantioselectivity and used as a learning dataset to build a model.
As noted above, in the original 2006-study, nine single point mutations of ANEH were experimentally evolved and assessed for their impact on the enantioselectivity $^{49}$ .
After 5 iterative rounds of saturation mutagenesis, a total of nine single point mutations led to an improved variant (LW202) with an E-value of 115.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Nine single mutants available; 28 recombinants previously reported.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Multiple mutants will show non-additive effects (epistasis).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze 28 multiple point mutants for epistasis by comparing experimental vs. additive predictions.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All 28 recombinants show epistasis (9 positive, 19 negative).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Build model DSA_FFT to capture epistasis.

evidence.quote_or_span: It turned out that all of the 28 variants with multiple mutations have a positive or a negative epistasis: 9 with positive epistasis and 19 with negative epistasis
It turned out that all of the 28 variants with multiple mutations have a positive or a negative epistasis: 9 with positive epistasis and 19 with negative epistasis (Table S4).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Epistasis confirmed; need model to capture non-additive effects.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can innov'SAR with FFT predict epistatic interactions?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Model DSA_FFT trained on 9 single mutants will outperform additive model for recombinants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build DSA_FFT using AAindex ZHOH040101 and FFT; evaluate with LOOCV on 28 recombinants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: DSA_FFT gives better predictions than additive model for 18 of 28 mutants, capturing epistasis.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Improve model by including multiple mutants in training set.

evidence.quote_or_span: The innov'SAR model allows a distinct capture of epistasis for these mutants
Among the 28 multiple point mutants, the model DSA\_FFT gives better results for 18 mutants than the prediction based only on the addition of mutations (Fig.

After the evaluation of multiple encoding indexes by innov'SAR, described previously, the encoding index ZHOH040101 $^{54}$ was identified as one of the best to generate an accurate model by innov'SAR and selected to build the model DSA\_FFT.
n=9 single point mutants of the enzyme were experimentally assessed for their enantioselectivity and used as a learning dataset to build a model.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DSA_FFT captures some epistasis but limited by training on singles only.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can including multiple mutants in training improve epistasis capture?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Adding 28 recombinants to training set (total 37 mutants) will improve accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build DSB_FFT on 37 mutants (9 singles + 28 multiples); evaluate with LOOCV.
evolution_trajectory.observation: DSB_FFT achieves LOOCV R²=0.96, better than DSA_FFT; predicts 22 of 28 recombinants better than additive.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test necessity of FFT by ablation.

evidence.quote_or_span: the addition of multiple point mutants to the learning dataset includes some information about the epistasis between mutations and thus improves the accuracy
the higher $R^{2}$ value (0.96) for the LOOCV training set
Among the 28 multiple point mutants, the model DSB\_FFT generates more accurate predictions for 22 mutants, better results than the one based only on the addition of mutations (see Fig.

We created a validation dataset with the 28 multiple point mutants (Table S3), including the mutant LW202, that were previously reported (among a total of 512) $^{50}$ , in order to check the accuracy of our models based on only 9 single point mutations.

Based on combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ ), the enantioselectivity of these 512 variants were predicted, and candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.
With only 37 mutants, our model was able to predict the potential outcome of 512, which means that on the basis of only 7% of the mutants, a whole fitness landscape was predicted.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DSB_FFT shows high accuracy; need to confirm FFT's role.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is FFT essential for epistasis prediction?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Removing FFT will reduce predictive accuracy.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Build DSB_noFFT model (same training set, no FFT) and compare.
evolution_trajectory.observation: DSB_noFFT achieves R²=0.88, lower than DSB_FFT (0.96); FFT improves epistasis capture.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate that only FFT-based model can find mutants better than LW202.

evidence.quote_or_span: This model has 0.88 as value of $R^{2}$
the higher $R^{2}$ value (0.96) for the LOOCV training set

In this case the innov'SAR application comprising FFT was the only method that could predict any improved recombinants, while a pure additive model and innov'SAR without FFT proposed at best only the already known experimental LW202 mutant.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DSB_noFFT less accurate; need to confirm FFT's unique ability to identify improved mutants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can additive or noFFT models predict mutants better than LW202?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Only FFT-based model can predict variants exceeding LW202 (E=115).
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare predictions from additive model, DSB_noFFT, and DSB_FFT for all 512 variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Additive model (R²=0.62) and noFFT (R²=0.64) fail to predict any mutant better than LW202; only DSB_FFT succeeds.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use DSB_FFT to predict and rank all 512 possible variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: FFT is an improving factor for the predictions of mutants with epistasis in the models generated by innov'SAR.
the predictions based on only the addition of mutations cannot find any better mutants than the mutant LW202
The prediction based solely on descriptors without FFT also failed to give predictions of enantioselectivity better than mutant LW202
We found 0.62 and 0.64 as $R^{2}$ values, respectively, for the predictions based on the addition of mutations and for the predictions generated without FFT.
The incorporation of the 28-multiple mutation variants, including the mutant LW202, into the dataset A should improve the accuracy of the model for the prediction of the 512 possible variants.

We could identify and experimentally confirmed extremely fit mutants out of 512 possible mutational combinations by using a dataset made of only $7\%$ of the mutants contained in the total fitness landscape.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: DSB_FFT validated as best model; ready for large-scale prediction.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which combinations of the 9 mutations yield highest enantioselectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: DSB_FFT can rank all 512 possible variants by predicted E-value.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Computationally generate all 2^9=512 variants and predict E-values using DSB_FFT.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 474 new variants predicted; top candidates identified for experimental validation.

evidence.quote_or_span: we generated computationally all combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ variants)
This approach resulted in $2^{9}-38=474$ new variants with multiple point mutations.

Based on combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ ), the enantioselectivity of these 512 variants were predicted, and candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do predicted high-E-value mutants actually show improved enantioselectivity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Experimentally measured E-values will match predictions.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct and assay P1-P5 mutants for enantioselectivity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All 5 mutants show properties matching predictions; P4 (E=253) and P5 (E=228) exceed LW202 (E=115); experimental vs. predicted R²=0.94.

evidence.quote_or_span: We were able to demonstrate that the 5 mutants have properties that corresponded quite well to the predictions.
Especially impressive is the performance of the predicted mutants P4(8) and P5(9), the experimental E-values amounting to 253 and 228, respectively
a $R^{2}$ value of 0.94 was obtained
These are distinctly better than mutant LW202 (E = 115) evolved in our original study.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does this confirm innov'SAR as a valid method for enantioselectivity engineering?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: innov'SAR can reliably predict improved enantioselective variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that innov'SAR with FFT successfully identified better mutants from only 7% of fitness landscape.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Better mutants with higher enantioselectivity found; method validated.

evidence.quote_or_span: candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.
Predictions of new improved versions of the epoxide hydrolase enzyme for enantioselectivity have been confirmed experimentally.

Based on combinations of the 9 single point mutations ( $2^{9}$ ), the enantioselectivity of these 512 variants were predicted, and candidates were experimentally checked: better mutants with higher enantioselectivity were indeed found.

We could identify and experimentally confirmed extremely fit mutants out of 512 possible mutational combinations by using a dataset made of only $7\%$ of the mutants contained in the total fitness landscape.
With only 37 mutants, our model was able to predict the potential outcome of 512, which means that on the basis of only 7% of the mutants, a whole fitness landscape was predicted.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Method validated but limited to recombining known mutations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can innov'SAR predict effects of mutations not in training set?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Current approach is limited to learned mutations; future work needed for non-learned mutations.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Note limitation and propose future direction.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Application limited to recombining mutations from training set; predicting non-learned mutations is future work.

evidence.quote_or_span: In this particular case we have limited the application to recombine learned mutations which are contained in the train set.
In a next step we are considering going further and to test to what extent our approach is able to predict the properties of non-learned mutations on learned or non-learned positions.

validation_methods: Experimental testing of top predicted mutants
success_verification.final_conclusion: innov'SAR with FFT successfully predicted improved enantioselective variants of ANEH, with experimental E-values up to 253, surpassing previous best LW202 (E=115). The method captured epistasis and required only 7% of the fitness landscape for training.
limitations: Application limited to recombining mutations present in the training set; predicting non-learned mutations remains future work.
